articles.title: 'Monumental setback' for Modi's foreign policy: Congress on Pakistan's role in US-Iran peace moves
articles.description: US-Iran talks: Congress attacks Modi government as Pakistan hosts key negotiations, calling it a setback for India’s foreign policy. Jairam Ramesh cites Asim Munir’s US outreach, questions India’s diplomatic strategy amid regional tensions.

articles.title: Promised American dream jobs that never...: How an Indian-origin duo ran a fake H-1B visa scheme for 2.5 years
articles.description: H-1B visa fraud: Sampath Rajidi and Sreedhar Mada, both 51 and residents of Dublin in California's East Bay, pleaded guilty this week on charges of conspiracy to commit H-1B visa fraud

articles.title: TMC vs Himanta Biswa Sarma Erupts As Mamata Banerjee Row Sparks Election Commission Complaint
articles.description: A political clash has intensified in West Bengal after Trinamool Congress filed a complaint against Assam Chief Minister Himanta Biswa Sarma over alleged divisive remarks. The party accused him of communal statements and violating the model code, while Sarma hit back with allegations of infiltration. The controversy has heightened tensions ahead of elections, drawing attention to the Election Commission’s next move.

articles.title: Arvind Kejriwal Case Hearing Live Updates: Delhi HC allows Kejriwal to file response to CBI’s reply to recusal plea, treats it as ‘written submissions’
articles.description: The court will pronounce its judgment at 4:30 on Kejriwal and others’ plea seeking recusal of Justice Swarana Kanta Sharma from hearing the excise policy case.

articles.description: Iran's VP warns that security in the Strait of Hormuz isn't free, linking it to unrestricted Iranian oil exports. The warning follows the US seizure of an Iranian vessel, with Tehran vowing retaliation for the 'maritime piracy'.

articles.title: Tejashwi hits back at CM over women’s quota row
articles.description: In a striking rebuttal, Tejashwi Yadav addressed CM Samrat Choudhary's remarks regarding the women's reservation bill, emphasizing that the opposition stands firmly behind it. However, he did not shy away from pointing fingers at the BJP, alleging that they are leveraging the bill to advance their own political interests through delimitation.

articles.title: For the First Time: Direct flights between Israel and Argentina
articles.description: Israel and Argentina have launched direct flights connecting the two nations. Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and Argentine President Javier Milei announced the new route. This initiative aims to deepen economic cooperation, facilitate business, and encourage tourism. The flights will make travel between the countries faster and more accessible.

articles.title: Bulgaria votes: Pro-Russia alliance leads, coalition deadlock likely
articles.description: Bulgarians are voting in an early parliamentary election, the 8th in 5 years. Polls show former President Rumen Radev's pro-Russia alliance leading, but without a clear majority, making a coalition government uncertain and deadlock likely.

articles.title: RK Nagar Assembly Polls: DMK vs AIADMK in Jayalalithaa's bastion

articles.title: Ramdev calls bill's failure an insult, wanted 50% women's quota
articles.description: Yoga Guru Ramdev slammed the failure of the women's reservation bill as an 'insult to Nari Shakti', advocating for 50% quota. The bill failed to get a two-thirds majority in Lok Sabha, sparking a political blame game over its implementation.

articles.title: After women’s quota bill defeat, CM slams opposition
articles.description: In a passionate address, Bihar Chief Minister Samrat Choudhary expressed his outrage over the opposition's thwarting of the women's reservation bill, deeming it a 'dark stain on democracy.' He accused political adversaries, particularly Congress and RJD, of undermining the progress of women, vowing to hold them accountable at the polls.

articles.title: 'Do you eat onions?' PM Modi takes 'Jhalmuri break' during Bengal poll campaign - watch
articles.description: Amidst a high-stakes political campaign in West Bengal, Prime Minister Narendra Modi took a refreshing pause for a jhalmuri snack in Jhargram. He interacted with the vendor, inquiring about the price and confirming his liking for onions with a witty remark. This candid moment unfolded as the Prime Minister intensified his campaign efforts across the state.

articles.title: 'Ceasefire in the Middle East, especially in Lebanon, are meant to be broken', analyst says
articles.description: In the midst of a 10-day ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon, new rounds of talks are due to take place in Washington to determine whether the truce can be extended. However, for Aaron David Miller, Senior Fellow at the Carnegie Endowment for International Peace, ceasefires in the Middle East, particularly in Lebanon, are often “meant to be broken”, reflecting a deeply pessimistic outlook. He also notes growing frustration from Donald Trump over Israeli military activity, and argues that the Israel–Lebanon and US–Iran dynamics are closely interconnected,

articles.description: Join the Shankara Jayanthi celebrations at Srisailam on April 21, featuring special rituals and prayers honouring Adi Shankaracharya.
